title: Xylophone Moroccan Seffa
description: A roasted, cheese-pull Moroccan dish featuring zucchini, tandoor, and seffa form, perfect for adventurous foodies. This vegan-friendly recipe is sure to impress with its rich flavors and textures.

ingredients.name: tandoor flatbread
ingredients.notes: available at Middle Eastern markets or online

ingredients.name: seffa form
ingredients.notes: available at Middle Eastern markets or online

instructions: Tear the tandoor flatbread into smaller pieces and set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together ras el hanout and olive oil.
Add the sliced red onion and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Spread the mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until caramelized.
Meanwhile, slice the zucchinis into 1-inch thick rounds and cook in a separate skillet with a little oil until tender.
In a separate pan, toast the seffa form over medium heat until lightly browned.
Open the can of chickpeas and drain the liquid. Mash the chickpeas with a fork until they form a chunky paste.
Spread the chickpea paste on the toasted seffa form, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Top the chickpea paste with sliced zucchinis and roasted red onion mixture.
Place the vegan mozzarella on top and transfer to the oven for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
